capacity.value: 87,523 after the 2026 renovation (largest in Mexico and Latin America); held up to ~115,000 for the 1986 World Cup

wc_record.value: SUPERLATIVE (verify): first stadium ever to host three World Cups (1970, 1986, 2026) and the first to host three World Cup opening matches

finals_hosted.value: Hosted two World Cup finals: 1970 (Brazil 4-1 Italy — Pelé's third title) and 1986 (Argentina 3-2 West Germany — Maradona)

iconic_moments.value: Site of Maradona's 'Hand of God' and 'Goal of the Century' in the 1986 quarter-final vs England

renovation.value: Nearly two-year renovation completed March 2026; reopened 28 March 2026 with a Mexico 0-0 Portugal friendly
